article_title: China's Orbital Chenguang Lines Up $8.4 Billion to Build a Gigawatt Data Center in Sun-Synchronous Orbit
summary: Substantively excellent Orbital Chenguang piece — SpaceNews and Xinhua claims verify verbatim — but two attribution issues: Three-Body Computing Constellation misattributed to Space.com (it's in Xinhua); operator named as 'ADA Space and Zhejiang Lab' but Xinhua attributes the launch to Zhejiang Lab; 'Carbon Credits noted' reference has no URL/citation in source set.

findings.message: Misattribution: 'ADA Space and Zhejiang Lab launched the first 12 satellites of the Three-Body Computing Constellation in May 2025' attributed to Space.com
findings.details: Space.com snapshot has zero mentions of ADA Space, Three-Body, Zhejiang, or computing constellation. The May 14 2025 launch of the first 12 'Three-Body Computing Constellation' satellites is in Xinhua (source-2), which attributes the project to Zhejiang Lab, not ADA Space + Zhejiang Lab jointly. Re-attribute to Xinhua and correct the operator (Zhejiang Lab) — ADA Space appears in Xinhua as a separate operator running the 2,800-satellite plan and Prometheus cloud.

Article: 'Carbon Credits noted that some lifecycle research suggests orbital systems could produce significantly higher total emissions than terrestrial alternatives'. There is no Carbon Credits URL in the sources list and no snapshot for this outlet. Remove the claim or add a proper citation with a snapshot.

content_preview.title: China's Orbital Chenguang Lines Up $8.4 Billion to Build a Gigawatt Data Center in Sun-Synchronous Orbit
content_preview.summary: Beijing-backed Orbital Chenguang secured $8.4 billion in credit lines from twelve Chinese banks to build a gigawatt-scale orbital computing constellation by 2035, opening a state-aligned third front against SpaceX and Blue Origin in the race to put AI compute in orbit.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A Beijing-incubated startup has become the first Chinese company to formally enter the orbital data center race at scale, securing 57.7 billion yuan ($8.4 billion) in strategic credit lines from a dozen major Chinese banks. Beijing Orbital Twilight Technology Co., Ltd., known as Orbital Chenguang, announced the completion of its Pre-A1 equity round on April 20 alongside the credit framework, [according to SpaceNews](https://spacenews.com/china-backs-orbital-data-center-startup-with-...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
Re-attribute the Three-Body Computing Constellation launch to Xinhua and correct operator to Zhejiang Lab.
Remove or properly cite the 'Carbon Credits noted' lifecycle-emissions reference.

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Space News piece (~950 words, in News range). Detailed structure, useful comparison to SpaceX/Blue Origin/Starcloud. Multiple internal cross-references valid (SpaceX FCC million-satellite filing, Blue Origin 51,600 satellites, Starcloud unicorn).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 snapshots. SpaceNews verifies all 8 claims attributed to it: 57.7B yuan / $8.4B / 12 banks named, Pre-A1 April 20, Beijing Astro-future Institute incubator, 24-org consortium, named equity investors, dawn-dusk orbit at 700-800 km, 3-phase 2025-2027 / 2028-2030 / 2035 plan, Chenguang-1 not flown, Ceres-2 + Tianlong-3 debut failures. Xinhua verifies all 4 claims: 250B yuan / \$36.6B by 2030 industry projection (CAICT), ADA Space 2,800-satellite plan (2,400 inference + 400 training), 100k-petaflop / million-petaflop targets, Prometheus cloud platform launch (Mar 26 2026 — slightly more specific than article's '2026'). Space.com verifies CASC five-year plan inclusion. Two errors: Three-Body Constellation misattributed to Space.com (it's in Xinhua + operator is Zhejiang Lab), Carbon Credits unsourced.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Substantively the article is correct, but 2 attribution issues need fixing.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, analytical. Distinguishing the Chinese state-incubated model from Western VC-funded model is good editorial framing.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent strategic-infrastructure piece. Two attribution fixes and ready for re-review.
